steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage has two independent clauses: one about travelers flying out of urban airports with increasing frequency, and one giving San Diego as a specific example. The blank must punctuate the end of the first clause and introduce the example correctly.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "frequency, for example.": This places "for example" at the end of the general sentence instead of before the San Diego example, so the transition is misplaced. ✗
  • "frequency, for example;": The semicolon separates the general statement from the San Diego example after "for example" has already been attached to the wrong clause, so the transition is misplaced. ✗
  • "frequency. For example,": A period ends the first independent clause cleanly. 'For example,' then opens the second sentence as a transitional phrase followed by a comma — the standard punctuation pattern. ✓
  • "frequency, for example,": Two commas around 'for example' while joining two independent clauses creates a comma splice. ✗
That matches the correct answer — C is correct.
